verdict.visual_pleasure: Judged against `editorial-balanced`: the Field Notes envelope lands cleanly — cream paper, Fraunces serif heads with italic terracotta accent, DM Sans body at a comfortable 760px measure, restrained pull-quotes and a single tinted TLDR card. The register feels right for a craft how-to — warmer than `formal-brief`, more disciplined than `playful-explainer`. One genuine blemish: every quoted source paragraph begins with a literal `>` character (markdown blockquote syntax leaked through the renderer), which interrupts the otherwise calm reading rhythm. Score 4 rather than 5 because of that artifact.
verdict.navigation: Judged against `editorial-balanced`: a sticky-top horizontal nav with four chapter anchors (Front matter / Starter / Dough / Bake) is the correct shape for an 870-word linear how-to — a left-rail TOC would have been overkill, a floating mini-nav would have been twee. Jump-links work, the bar stays out of the way while scrolling, and the section eyebrows (§) reinforce orientation. No scroll-spy active state and no in-page progress indicator, which is why this is a 4 and not a 5.
verdict.comprehension: Answered 10/10 cleanly using only the HTML. Every mechanism (yeasts + LAB), every ratio (500/375/10/100, 75% hydration), every timing (5–6 days, 30 min autolyse, 4–6 hr bulk, 8–16 hr retard, 250 °C × 45 min preheat, 20 + 20–25 min bake, ≥1 hr cool), and every failure mode (nail-polish smell, sideways spread, gummy crumb) is preserved verbatim in the source-quoted paragraphs. Nothing was lost in condensation.
verdict.illustration: Judged against `editorial-balanced`: three illustrations, all earning their space. The flour-to-loaf timeline SVG with dashed terracotta arrows externalizes the day-scale pacing better than prose could. The four-ingredient card grid makes the canonical ratio scannable. The FAQ accordion at the end of The Dough is a smart troubleshooting recap that doubles as retrieval practice. The card icons are generic check-circles (the only weak note), so 4 not 5.
verdict.books_finished_rate: Yes — emphatically. This is a tight, mechanism-first field guide where every claim survived with its 'why'. I could bake a loaf from this HTML alone with no anxiety about missing tribal knowledge. For a ~750-word source the condensation ratio is modest, but the rendering adds real navigability, a usable timeline, and a troubleshooting recap — net, I would skip the original and trust this for 100% of working-knowledge purposes.
